status.name: FastMath 6-12 (proposed)

status.replaces:
Nothing — gap in the current sequence.

status.passes_test: [AI guess — verify] Prediction: mastery = 85% accuracy at Springmath's CQPM (correct digits per minute) standard per skill; instrument = Springmath benchmarks. Parent-recognizable framing owed by owner. (source: Astro Math spec)
status.xp_hours: [AI guess — verify] 1.5-2.5 hours total to master all 76 skills (~2 min per skill). XP: 1 XP per minute over 80% accuracy. (source: Astro Math spec)

Janna + Zach Groshell (6/5, logged by Nils Lang): the need is continuous fluency diagnosis/maintenance beyond 5th grade; gamification must be encouraging, not punishing ("you are in 8th grade but we're putting you back in 2nd grade is not a good message"); "will be part of Alpha fluency stack for next school year." status.passes_test: [AI guess — verify] Prediction: target instrument is NWEA MAP Growth Math; K pilot evidence: prior score 66.5% → 93.6% by final mastery test (96.7% with the numeric-entry fix). Threshold vs MAP norm to be stated by owner. (sources: brainlift; Worksmart 6/5)

status.name: SuperBuilders Incept
status.subject: Math
status.replaces:
Zearn G3 base, Math Academy G5 base (G4 already on Blu Math) — see app_stack + https://timeback-loops-k8.vercel.app/course-sequence

Where it lands: Ships as "Blu Math" — the next-year Math base for G3-5 (already the now-era base at G4). Owners-sheet note says Grades 3-8 Math incl. mastery gates ~10 questions.
status.parent_summary: [AI guess — verify] Blu Math is a daily math course for grades 3-5 that promises to get your child ahead by two grade levels in five months, or your money back. A tutor sees a child 1-2 hours per week; Blu Math works with them 12-15. In the pilot, 4 of 5 students passed the Texas end-of-course math test on their first try, averaging 94% — one scored a perfect 100. The typical student finishes a full grade of math in about 13 hours of focused work. (draft from the team's own materials — owner to verify and complete the six answers)
status.standards_covered: [AI guess — verify] TEKS covered (+2,100 TEKS-level questions, first student passed TEKS place-out at 90%); state-configurable test prep claimed for all 50 states; CCSS/California frameworks loaded in Incept v2. NOT-covered half unstated — owner to complete.
status.passes_test: [AI guess — verify] Prediction: students completing Blu Math pass the state end-of-course math test at 90%+ on the first attempt, at ~3x the current first-time pass rate — the team's own parent-facing promise is "gets your child ahead by two grade levels in five months, or your money back." Pilot evidence: 4 of 5 TEKS EOC takers passed at 94% average (one 100%); first place-out at 90%. Baseline vs Math Academy to be measured. (sources: LevelMath brainlift; Worksmart 6/3)
status.xp_hours: [AI guess — verify] Median 13 hours total time to complete the G4 course; median total XP 1,200 from scratch (Worksmart 5/1). Median student finishes 4th-grade math in 6.2 weeks; 9 finish within 4 weeks, 18 within 8. Variable-XP A/B (4/8): +57% questions, +38% time on task, accuracy flat-to-up (76.5→77.1%).
status.effective_for: [AI guess — verify] G3-5 signed off to replace Math Academy for Fall 2026 (Worksmart 6/17, after meeting with Andy, Janna, academics). Named evidence: Noah Noorani (100% EOC after failing ~1/3 of Math Academy lessons); a TSA student who failed 11 times on Math Academy+Edia+hole-filling passed after 4 days on the app. 93.6% lesson rescue rate; 88.4% prerequisite-review pass rate.
status.notes: Worksmart (7/14 pull): stage 6-Full Deployment, lead Liwei Mao (team 7). Ships as Blu Math / Blu.School. Timeline (6/15): V2 G3-5 June 30; G6-8 content July; G9-12 before Aug 15. Brainlifts: workflowy levelmath-productmar (primary), levelmath-atoms-moat. Educator quote: "The students using the new math app are saying it teaches them much better."

brainlift.insight: Academic Projects replace existing app/course cells in the PowerPath course sequence with apps/courses that deliver better academic outcomes. This database is the agreed master data source for all of them.

The north-star question: what are the changes to the course sequence for the 2026-2027 school year that will be ready for students on August 1st? Answer = sy2026_27_changes (projects) + stack_changes (per-cell now→next diff).

Gaps are the product. Publishing missing/incorrect data to each subject owner and Learning Science sponsor is how it gets fixed — owners and sponsors fix the data themselves, no project managers tracking anyone down.
Delivering for students is its own best reward: this source highlights successes AND failures in delivering better learning outcomes. Do not soften either.

This is a raw data source, not a translated one. Frontier AI reasons under uncertainty with incomplete data better than any human — hand the calling LLM pure data, never interpretation layered on top.
The current production course sequence (what "now" means, and what projects replace) is live at https://timeback-loops-k8.vercel.app/course-sequence.

Approval flow, in order: plan_approved_by_ai → approved_by_learning_science → ready_for_students (owner) → approved_by_andy → approved_by_campus_dris → approved_by_guides. release_date is the owner's prediction of all-approved.
Improve me: file improvement_requests rows (any calling LLM or human can). They are stack-ranked and worked as the data source's own improvement loop.

Every course must answer six questions (the parent/customer summary is these answers rephrased in parent language): subject+grades covered; which 3rd-party standards it covers AND does not cover; the parent-recognizable standardized test passed at what threshold on completing the course(s) and their mastery gates; the entry mastery gate and threshold; XP hours to complete (median / knows-it-all / knows-nothing entrant, XP per focused minute, whether XP is farmable); and which students it works for — named Alpha students with 1-2 week falsifiable hypotheses.
brainlift.source: Questions every course needs to answer — WF 82041e9b (Academics Root)

improvements.request: app_stack has old/now/next matrices for Math only. Owners of Reading, Language, Science, Writing, Vocabulary, Social Studies, FastMath should each fill their subject's next-year matrix (set_stack_cell or send the grid) so north-star's per-cell diff covers every subject.

Fill the 'Questions every course needs to answer' brainlift (Workflowy node 9fe971ca6644, under Parent and Customer facing summaries in the spec). It is an empty placeholder; every project's parent_summary is supposed to answer it, so its content gates the parent_summary field for all 8 projects.
improvements.notes: Wrong premise: the brainlift is NOT empty. Canonical content lives at WF 82041e9b-aac7-b393-ce2f-929b0e407c72 (Academics Root) with worked examples (SS G3, AlphaFluency, Scribble). The spec node 9fe971ca6644 is a stub pointer.
